---
toc: false
---

# Tenacity {.unnumbered}

Tenacity is the first guided activity group in this book. These chapters help you get oriented to RStudio, practice moving between instructions and software, and begin building a reliable workflow for learning R.

In this section, the goal is not speed. The goal is to slow down enough to notice where you are, what you typed, what happened, and what you can check next.

## Chapters in this section

::: {.section-card-grid}

::: {.section-card}
### Chapter 1: Navigation

Get oriented to the RStudio interface, including the Console, Environment, Files pane, and basic workflow habits.

[Open Chapter 1](ch01-navigation.html){.section-button}
:::

::: {.section-card}
### Chapter 2: Scripts

Practice writing, saving, running, and submitting R scripts so your work is easier to reuse and revise.

[Open Chapter 2](ch02-scripts.html){.section-button}
:::

:::

## What to focus on

As you work through Tenacity, pay attention to three habits:

- where you are working in RStudio,
- whether you typed the code exactly as intended,
- and how you check what happened before moving on.

These habits will matter in every later chapter.
